creator: jens.kassel@servicefactory.com
text:
(In reply to Cy Schubert from comment #8)

Don't have ipnat.conf/ipf.conf. It is configured dynamically from a process. Here is an example of how it looks like:

# ipfstat -io
block out quick on em1 from any to 10.10.0.0/25 head 2
block out quick on em1 from any to 10.10.0.128/25 head 4
block out quick on em1 from any to 2.0.0.0/24 head 6
# Group 2
pass out quick on em1 from 10.0.0.6/32 to 10.10.0.0/25 group 2
pass out quick on em1 from any to 10.10.0.12/32 group 2
pass out quick on em1 from any to 10.10.0.11/32 group 2
pass out quick on em1 from any to 10.10.0.0/25 group 2
# Group 4
pass out quick on em1 from 10.0.0.6/32 to 10.10.0.128/25 group 4
pass out quick on em1 from any to 10.10.0.128/25 group 4
# Group 6
pass out quick on em1 from 10.0.0.6/32 to 2.0.0.0/24 group 6
pass out quick on em1 from any to 2.0.0.0/24 group 6
block in quick on em0 proto tcp from any to 192.168.120.93/32 port = mdqs
pass in quick on em0 proto tcp from 192.168.121.123/32 to 192.168.120.93/32 port = mdqs
block in quick on em1 proto udp from any to 10.10.0.255/32 port = netbios-ns
block in quick on em1 proto udp from any to 10.10.0.255/32 port = netbios-dgm
pass in quick on em1 proto udp from any to 10.10.0.1/32 port != sip
pass in quick on em1 proto tcp from any to 10.10.0.1/32
pass in quick on em1 proto icmp from any to 10.10.0.1/32
pass in quick on em1 proto esp from any to 10.10.0.1/32
pass in quick on em1 proto tcp/udp from any to 10.0.0.6/32 port != domain
pass in quick on em1 proto icmp from any to 10.0.0.6/32
block in quick on em1 from 10.10.0.0/25 to any head 1
block in quick on em1 from 10.10.0.128/25 to any head 3
block in quick on em1 from 2.0.0.0/24 to any head 5
block in quick on em1 from any to any
# Group 1
pass in quick on em1 from 10.10.0.12/32 to any group 1
pass in quick on em1 from 10.10.0.11/32 to any group 1
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from 10.10.0.12/32 to any port = https group 1
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from 10.10.0.12/32 to any port = http group 1
pass in quick on em1 proto tcp/udp from any to 10.0.0.6/32 port = domain group 1
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from any to any port = http group 1
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from any to any port = https group 1
# Group 3
pass in quick on em1 from 10.10.0.128/25 to 172.217.22.163/32 group 3
pass in quick on em1 from 10.10.0.128/25 to 194.68.78.65/32 group 3
pass in quick on em1 proto tcp/udp from any to 10.0.0.6/32 port = domain group 3
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from any to any port = http group 3
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from any to any port = https group 3
# Group 5
pass in quick on em1 from 2.0.0.0/24 to 172.217.22.163/32 group 5
pass in quick on em1 from 2.0.0.0/24 to 194.68.78.65/32 group 5
pass in quick on em1 proto tcp/udp from any to 10.0.0.6/32 port = domain group 5
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from any to any port = http group 5
block in quick on em1 to lo0 proto tcp from any to any port = https group 5

# ipnat -l
List of active MAP/Redirect filters:
map lo3 10.10.0.0/25 -> 127.0.0.3/32 portmap tcp/udp 9000:65500
map lo3 10.10.0.128/25 -> 127.0.0.3/32 portmap tcp/udp 9000:65500
map lo3 2.0.0.0/24 -> 127.0.0.3/32 portmap tcp/udp 9000:65500
map lo0 10.10.0.0/25 -> 127.0.0.1/32 portmap udp 9000:65500
map lo0 10.10.0.128/25 -> 127.0.0.1/32 portmap udp 9000:65500
map lo0 2.0.0.0/24 -> 127.0.0.1/32 portmap udp 9000:65500
map-block lo0 10.10.0.0/25 -> 127.0.0.1/32 ports 10 tcp
map-block lo0 10.10.0.128/25 -> 127.0.0.1/32 ports 10 tcp
map-block lo0 2.0.0.0/24 -> 127.0.0.1/32 ports 10 tcp
rdr lo0 0.0.0.0/0 port 80 -> 127.0.0.1 port 65501 tcp
rdr lo0 0.0.0.0/0 port 443 -> 127.0.0.1 port 65502 tcp
map lo1 10.10.0.0/25 -> 127.0.0.2/32 portmap tcp/udp 9000:65500
map lo1 10.10.0.128/25 -> 127.0.0.2/32 portmap tcp/udp 9000:65500
map lo1 2.0.0.0/24 -> 127.0.0.2/32 portmap tcp/udp 9000:65500


//Jens
